questionHtml: Calculate the value of the equilibrium constant, K, at 298 K. Use sections 1 and 2 of the data booklet.
markschemeHtml:
ln K=⟨⟨∆GR.T=⟩⟩ --330008.31x298 /«+»13.3 ✔

K = 6.13×105 ✔

 

Award [2] for correct final answer.

Accept answers in the range 4.4×105 to 6.2×105 (arises from rounding of ln K).

meta.title: Calculate the value of the equilibrium constant, K, at 298 K. Use sections 1 and 2 of the data booklet.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.3—How far? The extent of chemical change
Reactivity 2.3.7—The equilibrium constant and Gibbs energy change, ΔG, can both be used to measure the position of an equilibrium reaction.
